title: pilosella sulphurea subsp. pascuorum
intro: Pilosella sulphurea subsp. pascuorum, also known as yellow hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sunflower family that is native to Europe, Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Pilosella sulphurea subsp. pascuorum include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and skin irritations.
